2026-04-08T15:52:40.7432418Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-08T15:52:40.7432782Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-08T15:52:40.7433096Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m

no actual code snippets demonstrate package-specific operations (start, deallocate, hibernate, or the new Flex create)\\n- **Key concepts section is a stub** — the `Key concepts` section contains only a link to general Azure SDK docs; it does not explain the Flex provisioning model, the distinction between Submit vs. Execute operations, or the newly introduced concepts (priority types, VM size profiles, zone allocation policies)\\n- **No Samples test file for `ExecuteCreateFlex`** — `tests/Samples/` has no file covering this new operation; the only scenario coverage is in `tests/Scenario/ComputescheduleOperationsTests.cs`, which is not surfaced in the README via snippet placeholders\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/computeschedule/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeSchedule/README.md` — **edit**\\n\\nReplace the `Key concepts` section with substantive content:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n### Operation types\\n\\n**Execute Type Operations** run immediately on targeted virtual machines. The `VirtualMachinesExecuteCreateFlex` operation (added in `2026-03-01-preview`) belongs to this group and provisions VMs on demand.\\n\\n**Submit Type Operations** schedule an action up to 14 days in the future (e.g., `VirtualMachinesSubmitStart`, `VirtualMachinesSubmitDeallocate`, `VirtualMachinesSubmitHibernate`).\\n\\n### Flex provisioning model (`VirtualMachinesExecuteCreateFlex`)\\n\\nThe Flex create operation supports:\\n\\n- **Priority types** (`ComputeSchedulePriorityType`): `Spot` for preemptible capacity at reduced cost, or `Regular` for standard on-demand capacity.\\n- **VM size profiles** (`ComputeScheduleVmSizeProfile`): An ordered list of VM sizes to try when provisioning; the service works through the list until a size is successfully allocated.\\n- **Allocation strategy** (`ComputeScheduleAllocationStrategy`): Controls whether to use `LowestPrice`, `CapacityOptimized`, or `PriceCapacityOptimized` when selecting from the VM size profiles.\\n- **Zone preferences** (`ComputeScheduleZonePreference`): Optionally specify preferred availability zones and a zone allocation policy.\\n- **OS type** (`ComputeScheduleOSType`): `Windows` or `Linux`.\\n\\n```\\n\\nReplace the `Examples` section to include a snippet placeholder for the new Flex operation in addition to a pointer to the samples page:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nThe following example shows how to use `VirtualMachinesExecuteCreateFlex` to provision a VM with Spot priority and multiple fallback sizes.\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\nAdditional code samples are available on the [Azure SDK for .NET samples page]((aka.ms/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n**Create** `sdk/computeschedule/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeSchedule/tests/Samples/Sample_ExecuteCreateFlex.cs`\\n\\nThe file should contain a test class with a method demonstrating `VirtualMachinesExecuteCreateFlex`. Wrap the core sample code in `#region Snippet:ComputeScheduleExecuteCreateFlex` / `#endregion`. Any assertions, cleanup, or test-specific infrastructure that should not appear in the README must be wrapped in `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif`.\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Collections.Generic;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Core;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeSchedule;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeSchedule.Models;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeSchedule.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample_ExecuteCreateFlex : SamplesBase\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task ExecuteCreateFlexSample()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ComputeScheduleExecuteCreateFlex\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string location = \\u0026#34;eastus\\u0026#34;;\\n\\n            var flexProperties = new ComputeScheduleFlexProperties(\\n                vmSizeProfiles: new List\\n                {\\n                    new ComputeScheduleVmSizeProfile(\\u0026#34;Standard_D2s_v3\\u0026#34;),\\n                    new ComputeScheduleVmSizeProfile(\\u0026#34;Standard_D4s_v3\\u0026#34;),\\n                },\\n                osType: ComputeScheduleOSType.Linux)\\n            {\\n                PriorityProfile = new ComputeSchedulePriorityProfile\\n                {\\n                    PriorityType = ComputeSchedulePriorityType.Spot,\\n                    AllocationStrategy = ComputeScheduleAllocationStrategy.CapacityOptimized,\\n                },\\n            };\\n\\n            var content = new ExecuteCreateFlexContent(\\n                flexProperties: flexProperties,\\n                virtualMachineResourceIds: new List\\n                {\\n                    new ResourceIdentifier($\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/my-rg/providers/Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/my-vm\\u0026#34;),\\n                },\\n                correlationId: Guid.NewGuid().ToString());\\n\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = client.GetSubscriptionResource(\\n                new ResourceIdentifier($\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\\u0026#34;));\\n            Response result =\\n                await subscription.VirtualMachinesExecuteCreateFlexAsync(\\n                    location: location,\\n                    content: content);\\n\\n            foreach (var resourceOperation in result.Value.Results)\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;VM: {resourceOperation.ResourceId}, Status: {resourceOperation.OperationStatus}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nAfter writing the file, add the snippet placeholder to the README as shown in Step 1.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at
